title: Ticketly House Style
description: How a team writes tickets: the effort rubric (what each Fibonacci point means), the default ticket-ID prefix vocabulary to suggest from, the tone rules for titles/descriptions/acceptance criteria, and a pointer to a few-shot example backlog. Ticketly reads this so generated tickets match the team's voice. A default ships in house-style/default.json; a project may override it.

effort_rubric.description: What each Fibonacci point means for this team, so 'M' means the same thing every time. Epics (0) are sized by their children and are not listed here.

prefix_vocab.description: The team's default ticket-ID prefix vocabulary. Ticketly suggests from these (filtered to the project's actual layers) and the user confirms. Project-specific domain prefixes can be added at profile time.

domain.description: What this prefix groups, e.g. 'Architecture & core conventions'.

domain_specific.description: True for prefixes tied to a particular product domain (e.g. OCR). Suggested only when the project clearly has that domain.

tone.description: Plain-language rules describing how each field should read, derived from the team's real tickets.

few_shot.description: Pointer to a worked example backlog (valid against the ticket schema) that demonstrates the team's voice. Used as few-shot during generation.

backlog.description: Repo-relative path to the example backlog JSON, e.g. 'examples/house-style-backlog.json'.
